steps.title: Expand the two binomials
steps.content:
Multiply $(x-\frac{3}{2})(x+14)$ using FOIL:
$$x \cdot x + x \cdot 14 - \frac{3}{2} \cdot x - \frac{3}{2} \cdot 14 = x^2 + 14x - \frac{3}{2}x - 21$$
Combine the middle terms: $14x - \frac{3}{2}x = \frac{28}{2}x - \frac{3}{2}x = \frac{25}{2}x$.
So $(x-\frac{3}{2})(x+14) = x^2 + \frac{25}{2}x - 21$.

steps.title: Match against the answer choices
steps.content:
• A. $x^2+25x-21$ — leading coefficient is 1, not 2; eliminated.
  • B. $2x^2+11x-21$ — constant is $-21$ instead of $-42$; eliminated.
  • C. $2x^2+25x-42$ — matches our expansion exactly.
  • D. $2x^2-42$ — missing the $25x$ term; eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
